title: Good Neighbours and Jobs Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A person who lives near you.
rounds.explanation: neighbour: A person who lives near you.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Strong shoes that cover the feet and part of the legs.
rounds.explanation: boots: Strong shoes that cover the feet and part of the legs.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A person who grows food and looks after animals.
rounds.explanation: farmer: A person who grows food and looks after animals.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A person who finds out news and writes stories about it.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The special clothes people wear for a job.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Doing something to make things easier for someone.

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: My neighbour Nadia grows flowers in her garden.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The brave firefighter rescued the cat from the tree.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The firefighter wears a mask to breathe fresh air in smoke.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Which question asks about an action?
rounds.explanation: Use What to ask about an action.

rounds.prompt: The firefighters ___ climbing the ladder.
rounds.explanation: Firefighters means more than one person, so use are.

rounds.prompt: Which is the correct -ing form of drive?
rounds.explanation: Drop the final e in drive before adding -ing.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: Why do firefighters wear masks? Why do they wear gloves?
choices: Masks help firefighters breathe fresh air; gloves protect their hands from hot things.
The neighbours are helping one another.
Adam brought water, Selma swept, Adam carried boxes, and the firefighters helped clean Sarah's shop.
rounds.answer: Masks help firefighters breathe fresh air; gloves protect their hands from hot things.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Masks help firefighters breathe fresh air; gloves protect their hands from hot things.

rounds.prompt: On which street does Samira live? What is near it?
choices: The neighbours are helping one another.
They chose to help her after finishing their own important jobs, showing kindness and teamwork.
Samira lives on Warta Street, near the market.
rounds.answer: Samira lives on Warta Street, near the market.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Samira lives on Warta Street, near the market.

rounds.prompt: How did the neighbours help old Sarah after the fire?
choices: Anyone can notice a need and help kindly; helping is not limited to a person's paid job.
Adam brought water, Selma swept, Adam carried boxes, and the firefighters helped clean Sarah's shop.
Samira lives on Warta Street, near the market.
rounds.answer: Adam brought water, Selma swept, Adam carried boxes, and the firefighters helped clean Sarah's shop.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Adam brought water, Selma swept, Adam carried boxes, and the firefighters helped clean Sarah's shop.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 — Describe a Job
Speaking Game — Who Am I?
Speaking 5 - What Is Happening Now?
rounds.target: Describe six actions in the picture using is or are and an -ing word.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: My teacher helps us read new English words.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The teacher is teaching us new words.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: A doctor helps people who are sick.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'gloves' mean in this unit?
choices: Coverings for the hands, with a part for each finger.
A person who lives near you.
A person whose job is to clean windows.
rounds.answer: Coverings for the hands, with a part for each finger.
rounds.explanation: gloves: Coverings for the hands, with a part for each finger.

rounds.prompt: What does 'shopkeeper' mean in this unit?
choices: A person who lives near you.
A person whose job is to clean windows.
A person who sells things in a shop.
rounds.answer: A person who sells things in a shop.
rounds.explanation: shopkeeper: A person who sells things in a shop.

rounds.prompt: What does 'window cleaner' mean in this unit?
choices: A person who finds out news and writes stories about it.
A person whose job is to clean windows.
A person who lives near you.
rounds.answer: A person whose job is to clean windows.
rounds.explanation: window cleaner: A person whose job is to clean windows.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'nurse'?
rounds.explanation: nurse is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'growing'?
rounds.explanation: growing is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'firefighter'?
rounds.explanation: firefighter is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: A person who keeps people safe and helps them follow the law.
A person whose job is to drive a bus.
A hard hat that protects the head.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: A person who helps people who are sick.
A person who helps others learn.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: Doing something to make things easier for someone.
Making a car or bus go.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: The ____ asked questions about our market day.
rounds.explanation: The reporter asked questions about our market day.

rounds.prompt: Which spelling is correct?
rounds.explanation: The bus driver takes people from our town to the city.

rounds.prompt: Who helps people who are sick?
rounds.explanation: A doctor helps people who are sick.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: What does 'neighbour' mean?
choices: A person who lives near you.
A person whose job is to clean windows.
A person who keeps people safe and helps them follow the law.
A person who finds out news and writes stories about it.
rounds.answer: A person who lives near you.
rounds.explanation: My neighbour Nadia grows flowers in her garden.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A person whose job is to clean windows.
rounds.explanation: The window cleaner uses a long pole to reach the high windows.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'police officer'?
rounds.explanation: The police officer helped the children cross the busy road.
